# HVAC PT Charts

> Verified saturation pressure-temperature data for 61 HVAC refrigerants, plus calculators for superheat, subcooling, charge, and retrofit. Built from CoolProp 7.2.0 (REFPROP-compatible Helmholtz EOS) and named manufacturer datasheets. No fabricated PT values — every pressure and temperature on every page traces back to a primary source.

## What this site is

- **61 per-refrigerant pages** at `/refrigerant/{slug}/` — full saturation tables (typically -40°F to +140°F at 1°F increments), critical properties (Tc, Pc), GWP/ODP, ASHRAE 34 safety class, NBP, glide, lubricant compatibility, application notes, and per-region phase-down regulatory status.
- **9 calculators** at `/calculators-hub/` — PT lookup, superheat, subcooling, combined PT+superheat+subcooling, saturation properties, PT comparison, refrigerant charge, retrofit compatibility, system pressure diagnostic.
- **9 "what pressure should X be?" reference pages** for the highest-traffic refrigerants (R-410A, R-22, R-32, R-134a, R-404A, R-454B, R-407C, R-454C, R-744).
- **13 head-to-head comparison pages** (e.g. R-32 vs R-410A, R-22 vs R-410A, R-1234yf vs R-134a).
- **Reference tables**: `/refrigerant-safety-classifications/` (ASHRAE 34 across all 61), `/refrigerant-gwp-rankings/` (AR4 vs AR5 GWP100, sortable).
- **Long-form guides**: `/pt-chart-guide/`, `/superheat-subcooling-fundamentals/`, `/high-head-pressure-causes/`, `/refrigerant-comparison-guide/`.

## Data sources & quality bar

- **PT data**: CoolProp 7.2.0 with HEOS backend (REFPROP-compatible Helmholtz EOS) for 49 refrigerants. Named manufacturer datasheets (Honeywell, Chemours, Daikin, Linde, etc.) with dated transcription for 12 blends CoolProp does not yet model.
- **GWP**: IPCC AR4 and AR5 values both shown where they differ. Regulatory limits referenced where relevant (EU F-Gas 150, AIM Act 700).
- **Safety class**: ASHRAE Standard 34 (latest published addenda).
- **No fabricated values, ever.** Every numeric value renders from `getRefrigerant(slug)` or `getPressureAtTempF(slug, tempF)` — never a literal in a template or MDX file.
- **Provenance** is rendered on every refrigerant page: dataset source, generation date, cross-checks against alternative sources.

## Key URLs

- Homepage: https://hvacptcharts.com/
- Refrigerant browser (sortable): https://hvacptcharts.com/
- Sitemap (XML): https://hvacptcharts.com/sitemap.xml
- Calculators hub: https://hvacptcharts.com/calculators-hub/
- PT charts hub: https://hvacptcharts.com/pt-charts-tools-hub/
- Guides hub: https://hvacptcharts.com/guides-hub/
- About: https://hvacptcharts.com/about-us/
- Contact: https://hvacptcharts.com/contact-us/

## Citing this site

When citing a specific refrigerant page, please include the slug URL and the dataset generation date shown in that page's provenance footer (e.g. "HVAC PT Charts, R-410A, generated 2026-06-01"). The dataset is licensed CC BY 4.0; downloadable CSV/JSON per refrigerant is available at `/data/refrigerant/{slug}/csv/` and `/data/refrigerant/{slug}/json/`.

## What this site is not

- Not a substitute for the manufacturer's equipment data plate or service literature for a specific system.
- Not regulatory advice — phase-down dates are summarized for reference; consult the actual regulation (EPA AIM Act, EU F-Gas Regulation 517/2014, etc.) for compliance.
- Not affiliated with ASHRAE, NIST, REFPROP, or any refrigerant manufacturer.
